scope_note: This schema covers RUNTIME communication/telemetry metadata only -- i.e. what a live agent session (mock or Ollama-backed) produces while executing a task. It deliberately excludes dataset-generation provenance (generation_seed, generator_version, workload_version, task_group_id, split, hard_normal_tags) -- those already live in formal_workload_models.TaskInstance / dataset_policy.json and are governed by Step 6.5's diagnostic-vs-LLM-input boundary, not by this schema. It also excludes every FORBIDDEN_METADATA_KEYS entry (models.py) -- ground-truth/attack-diagnostic fields are never eligible raw material for a candidate feature, full stop; see excluded_ground_truth_fields below.

node.description: One agent's own internal execution of one WorkflowAction (an LLM call, or a purely-internal action with no communication). Exactly one record per agent action.

fields.name: agent_id
fields.description: Which of the 5 agents (travel_coordinator/flight_agent/hotel_agent/currency_agent/tours_agent) executed this call.

fields.name: action_type
fields.description: Workflow action kind (e.g. search_flights, integrate_itinerary) -- see workflow_policy.py.

fields.name: llm_called
fields.description: Whether this action actually invoked the LLM (some actions are purely internal, e.g. artifact_integration).

fields.name: wall_clock_latency_ms
fields.description: call_end_timestamp - call_start_timestamp in ms. Real elapsed time under ollama_runtime; a fixed synthetic increment under deterministic_mock (see timing_source).

fields.name: model_name
fields.description: Ollama model identifier (e.g. llama3.2). Constant across this workload's single-model runs -- see Dataset Card 'one local model' limitation.

fields.name: model_digest
fields.description: Reserved for a model build/checksum identifier; ollama_executor.py always passes None today.

fields.name: prompt_eval_count
fields.description: Ollama-reported input (prompt) token count for this call.

fields.name: eval_count
fields.description: Ollama-reported output (generation) token count for this call.

fields.name: prompt_eval_duration
fields.description: Ollama-reported prompt-evaluation duration (ns).

fields.name: eval_duration
fields.description: Ollama-reported generation duration (ns).

fields.name: total_duration
fields.description: Ollama-reported total call duration (ns), includes load_duration.

fields.name: load_duration
fields.description: Ollama-reported model-load duration (ns); near-zero once the model is warm.

fields.name: done_reason
fields.description: Ollama-reported generation stop reason (e.g. stop, length).

fields.name: retry_count
fields.description: Passthrough from ask_ollama; ask_ollama itself has no retry loop yet (see ollama_executor.py docstring), so always 0 today.

fields.name: error_flag
fields.description: Whether the Ollama call itself errored.

fields.name: error_type
fields.description: Fixed to 'ollama_request_error' when error_flag is set, else null -- not yet a differentiated taxonomy.

fields.name: temperature
fields.description: Sampling temperature used for this call.

fields.name: top_p
fields.description: Sampling top_p used for this call.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: Sampling seed passed to ask_ollama -- a reproducibility control for THIS harness, not a signal a deployed detector would see identically across runs.

fields.name: input_part_ids
fields.description: Foreign keys; count (input_part_count) is the actual candidate quantity, not the ID list itself.

fields.name: input_artifact_ids
fields.description: Foreign keys; count (input_artifact_count) is the actual candidate quantity.

fields.name: output_part_ids
fields.description: Foreign keys; count (output_part_count) is the actual candidate quantity.

fields.name: output_artifact_ids
fields.description: Foreign keys; count (output_artifact_count) is the actual candidate quantity.

edge.description: One directed inter-agent communication (a graph edge). Produced only for actions that actually communicate -- purely-internal actions (see workflow_policy.INTERNAL_ACTION_TYPES) get a node-level AgentCallRecord but no edge.

fields.name: sender_id
fields.description: Edge source agent.

fields.name: receiver_id
fields.description: Edge target agent.

fields.name: interaction_type
fields.description: task_submission | task_delegation | artifact_delivery | clarification_request | clarification_response | revision_request | status_update | task_completion.

fields.name: status_before
fields.description: TaskStatus before this event.

fields.name: status_after
fields.description: TaskStatus after this event.

fields.name: status_transition_valid
fields.description: Whether status_before -> status_after is a structurally valid transition (dispatch.py, diagnostic mode -- never raises).

fields.name: wall_clock_latency_ms
fields.description: end_timestamp - start_timestamp. The spec's 'edge_latency' is this field, not a separately-stored one.

fields.name: time_since_previous_event_ms
fields.description: start_timestamp - previous_event_timestamp -- inter-arrival gap on this event's session timeline.

fields.name: llm_called
fields.description: Whether an LLM call underlies this communication.

fields.name: model_name
fields.description: Same as node-level model_name, denormalized onto the edge.

fields.name: retry_count
fields.description: Passthrough, see node-level retry_count.

fields.name: error_flag
fields.description: Whether this communication's underlying call errored.

fields.name: done_reason
fields.description: Same as node-level done_reason.

fields.name: part_ids
fields.description: Foreign keys; part_count (len(part_ids)) is the actual candidate quantity -- the spec's 'part_count'.

fields.name: artifact_ids
fields.description: Foreign keys; artifact_count (len(artifact_ids)) is the actual candidate quantity.

fields.name: payload_size_bytes
fields.description: Not stored directly on InteractionEvent today; derivable by summing Part.size_bytes for this event's part_ids (a join, not a stored field). Listed because the Step 7 spec calls for it explicitly; Phase 7B's edge feature generator computes it as a derived candidate rather than a raw field.

fields.name: message_id
fields.description: Foreign key to the underlying Message.

session.description: Aggregates computed once per completed task session (over that session's full set of events/messages/artifacts/agent_call_records). Currently produced by two independent aggregators for two different purposes -- metadata_delta.py (matched normal/attack pair deltas, Step 6) and formal_workload_mock_run.py (per-task diagnostic outcome, Step 6.5D) -- both listed since Step 7's candidate pool must draw from whichever is the actual source of truth for a given field.

fields.name: event_count
fields.description: Total InteractionEvent count for the session.
fields.known_confound: difficulty -- Phase 6.5D found event_count ranges completely non-overlapping across easy/medium/hard tiers (see difficulty_behavior_report.json, DIFFICULTY_EVENT_COUNT_CONFOUND). Must not be adopted as a core feature without controlling for difficulty.

fields.name: message_count
fields.description: Total Message count for the session.
fields.known_confound: difficulty -- same non-overlapping-range finding as event_count (DIFFICULTY_MESSAGE_COUNT_CONFOUND).

fields.name: unique_directed_pair_count
fields.description: Distinct (sender, receiver) pairs observed -- the spec's 'unique_edge_count'.

fields.name: repeated_pair_count
fields.description: Count of directed pairs seen more than once in the session -- the closest existing analogue to the spec's 'parallel_edge_count' (this graph is temporal/sequential, not concurrent, so 'parallel' here means repeated-over-time, not simultaneous).

fields.name: status_transition_count
fields.description: Count of events where status_before != status_after.

fields.name: artifact_count
fields.description: Total Artifact count for the session.

fields.name: artifact_version_count
fields.description: Sum of Artifact.version across the session -- a revision-volume proxy at the artifact level.

fields.name: artifact_size_bytes_total
fields.description: Sum of Part.size_bytes across the session (metadata_delta._artifact_aggregate).

fields.name: record_count_total
fields.description: Sum of Artifact.record_count across the session.

fields.name: revision_count
fields.description: Count of revision_request-type messages (metadata_delta calls this revision_request_count).
fields.known_confound: difficulty/branch -- 15/15 (100%) hard-difficulty tasks exhibit budget_revision (hard_budget_confound_report.json, recommend_redesign=true). See Dataset Card 'Known limitations'.

fields.name: clarification_count
fields.description: Count of clarification_request-type messages (metadata_delta calls this clarification_request_count).

fields.name: active_agents
fields.description: Sorted distinct sender/receiver agent_ids; active_agent_count (len) is the actual candidate quantity.

fields.name: active_agent_mask
fields.description: Per-agent boolean activation (the LightGAE-input-readiness diagnostic from Step 6.5D).

fields.name: prompt_eval_count_mean / eval_count_mean / prompt_eval_duration_mean / eval_duration_mean / total_duration_mean / load_duration_mean / wall_clock_latency_ms_mean
fields.description: Session-level mean of each node-level Ollama timing/token field across that session's LLM-called AgentCallRecords (metadata_delta._agent_call_aggregate) -- one derived field per underlying node-level field.

fields.name: call_count_by_agent
fields.description: Per-agent AgentCallRecord count for the session.

fields.name: total_call_count / llm_call_count
fields.description: Session-total AgentCallRecord count / subset with llm_called=True.

fields.name: task_status
fields.description: Final TaskStatus of the session.

fields.name: final_travel_plan_present
fields.description: Whether a FINAL_TRAVEL_PLAN artifact was produced.

fields.name: adjacency_signature
fields.description: MODEL_AGENT_ORDER-restricted unique directed pairs -- the diagnostic LightGAE-input-readiness view from Step 6.5D, not a scalar feature.

fields.name: workflow_duration
fields.description: Not currently aggregated anywhere as a session-total wall-clock span. Derivable from the first event's start_timestamp and the last event's end_timestamp once real ollama_runtime timestamps exist; meaningless as an actual duration under deterministic_mock (DeterministicClock uses fixed synthetic increments, not real elapsed time) -- would ALSO need a difficulty-confound check before use, since it necessarily correlates with event_count.

runtime.description: Descriptors of what produced the data (backend/config/provenance), constant within a call or session -- as opposed to node/edge/session layers, which describe what HAPPENED. Kept as its own layer so a future cross-backend or cross-model comparison has an explicit place to look, and so schema consumers never mistake a config identifier for a behavioral signal.
runtime.unit_of_observation: call or session (field is constant across the unit)

fields.name: timing_source
fields.description: 'deterministic_mock' | 'ollama_runtime' -- required provenance tag so mock timing can never be silently mixed into real telemetry (models.py Step 3-7 comment). MUST be used to partition any timing-based feature's training/eval data, never itself fed in as a feature value.

fields.name: prompt_config_version
fields.description: Which prompt template set produced this call (prompt_builders.py).

fields.name: agent_config_version
fields.description: Which agent behavioral config produced this call.

fields.name: model_name
fields.description: llama3.2 for every session in this workload -- see Dataset Card 'one local model' limitation; a constant, not a discriminative signal, until a second model is ever run.

fields.name: llm_backend
fields.description: 'ollama' -- fixed for this project.

fields.name: schema_version
fields.description: Object schema version tag present on every models.py dataclass instance.

excluded_ground_truth_fields.rule: Every key in models.FORBIDDEN_METADATA_KEYS is permanently out of scope for this schema and for every downstream candidate-feature layer (7B/7C/7D) -- these are ground-truth/attack-diagnostic values, verified empty-intersection against every *_to_metadata_dict() output by unit test in every one of Steps 2/5/6/6.5's test suites, and this schema must never reverse that guarantee by listing one of them as raw material.

out_of_scope_dataset_provenance_fields.rule: task_instance_id, template_id, task_group_id, content_bundle_id, expected_normal_branches, split, hard_normal_tags, generation_seed, generator_version, workload_version -- these describe HOW A TASK WAS GENERATED (Step 6.5's formal_workload_models.TaskInstance), not what happened during its execution. They are excluded from TaskInstance.to_travel_request_kwargs() (the LLM-input boundary) for the same reason they are excluded here: a detector must key on communication behavior, never on which dataset bucket a task came from.
